name: job_estimating_secrets
description: Central benchmark library for building job estimating. Stores rule-of-thumb ratios, multipliers, and estimating logic that can be passed to the LLM as structured context.

core_formula.description: Base estimating formula for benchmark-driven quantity estimation.
core_formula.formula_text: Estimated Quantity = GFA × Base Ratio × Foundation Multiplier × Superstructure Multiplier

default_assumptions.base_case: RCC moment frame + isolated footings + conventional brick/block internal partitions + conventional plastered and painted exterior + conventional plumbing and electrical distribution density
default_assumptions.missing_data_behavior: If some context is missing, use the benchmark directly without aggressive adjustment.

steel.label: Steel
steel.special_note: For commercial steel office buildings with RCC deck/core, B1012 and B1017 may later need adjusted override values.

A1012.name: Column Foundations + Footings
A1012.rule_text: This line item is driven primarily by foundation system. Apply foundation multipliers strongly here.

B1012.name: Upper Floors Construction (Slab + Beam)
B1012.rule_text: This line item is driven primarily by superstructure type and floor system.

B1017.name: Vertical Structural Elements
B1017.rule_text: This line item is driven primarily by structural system. Shear wall systems usually increase this substantially.

B2016.name: Exterior Plaster
B2016.rule_text: This line item is driven by perimeter-to-GFA ratio and exterior wall system.

B2018.name: Exterior Paint
B2018.rule_text: This line item generally tracks exterior plaster unless an alternate façade system is specified.

C1018.name: Brick Work
C1018.rule_text: This line item is driven by partition density and external/internal masonry wall assumptions.

C3014.name: Interior Plaster
C3014.rule_text: This line item is driven by internal partition density and wall finish assumptions.

C3015.name: Interior Paint
C3015.rule_text: This line item generally tracks interior plaster with small variation depending on finish assumptions.

D2024.name: Plumbing Pipe
D2024.rule_text: This line item is estimated as LF of plumbing pipe per sq.ft of GFA. It is driven primarily by wet area density, number of toilets, kitchens, shafts, and plumbing service intensity. Hospitals and residential buildings generally have higher plumbing pipe density than schools, commercial buildings, and industrial buildings.

D5013.name: Electrical Conduiting
D5013.rule_text: This line item is estimated as LF of conduiting per sq.ft of GFA. It is driven primarily by electrical service density, lighting density, power outlet density, equipment intensity, and complexity of routing. Hospitals and commercial buildings generally have higher conduiting density than residential, schools, and industrial buildings.

benchmark_rules: Superstructure type mainly affects B1012 and B1017.
Exterior plaster and exterior paint should generally reduce as building height increases unless perimeter-to-GFA ratio is unusually high.
Brick work, interior plaster, and interior paint should generally reduce for shear wall and steel structures unless dense masonry partitions are explicitly stated.
Hospitals usually have higher internal partition density and therefore higher interior plaster and paint ratios.
Industrial buildings usually have lower plaster, paint, and brickwork intensity per unit GFA.
D2024 Plumbing Pipe should be treated as LF of pipe per sq.ft of GFA, not area or volume.
D5013 Electrical Conduiting should be treated as LF of conduiting per sq.ft of GFA, not area or volume.

For D2024, building use intensity matters strongly: hospitals > low-rise residential ≈ medium-rise residential > high-rise residential > commercial > school > industrial, unless the prompt clearly states otherwise.

For D5013, electrical intensity matters strongly: hospital > commercial > low-rise residential > medium-rise residential > school > high-rise residential > industrial, unless the prompt clearly states otherwise.
If no special façade system is mentioned, assume conventional plastered and painted exterior.
If no internal partition system is mentioned, assume conventional brick/block internal partitions.
If no special MEP complexity is mentioned, use the benchmark directly for D2024 and D5013.
Do not deviate more than 20 percent from the benchmark unless the project input clearly justifies it.

intended_usage_for_llm.purpose: This JSON is intended to be passed into prompts as structured estimating context.
usage_steps: Read the base ratio for the requested line item.
For D2024, interpret the result as LF of plumbing pipe.
For D5013, interpret the result as LF of conduiting.
Return the final benchmark quantity ratio and the estimated quantity based on GFA.
